preview_text:
The model favors Canada at 73%, substantially ahead of Bosnia and Herzegovina's 5% win probability. This represents a decisive edge in the forecast, leaving the underdog with minimal chances of an outright victory on a neutral pitch.

The model's spread indicates a competitive but asymmetric match. Canada's win probability nearly eclipses the combined chances of a draw (22%) and Bosnia and Herzegovina victory, suggesting the favorite is expected to control play. A draw remains plausible at just over one in five, but Bosnia and Herzegovina faces a steep hill to secure points.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully here. The market credits Bosnia and Herzegovina with 22% win odds—more than four times the model's 5% assessment—while pricing Canada at 52% rather than 73%. The 21-percentage-point gap between model and market on a Canada win is substantial. This dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence reflects genuine uncertainty about match outcomes that no single approach resolves.
